metadata.notes: Voice rewrite — conversational walkthrough style matching Wingspan gold standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, RulesPal, Dized rules, BGG forums.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
- 1 double-sided game board — use the 1–3 player side or the 4–5 player side depending on your count
- 5 asymmetric hive mats, each with hexagonal tile slots and resource storage spaces
- 5 docking mats with a Landing Area and an Active Pool section

#### Tiles (129 total, all unique)
- **Farm tiles** (hexagonal) — give you resource storage and income every time you retrieve workers
- **Recruit tiles** (hexagonal) — grant ongoing abilities that fire when you take specific actions
- **Development tiles** (hexagonal) — one-time immediate benefits when built
- **Carving tiles** — major end-game scoring based on conditions; limited supply, never replenished once taken
- **Planet tiles** — shuffled face-down, revealed when you explore
- **Dance tiles** — placed on Convert action spaces at setup
- **20 faction tiles** (double-sided: basic and upgraded) — each gives your hive a unique asymmetric power
- **Frame tiles** — 4-hex extensions you can bolt onto your hive mat for extra building space

#### Cards
- 45 seed cards — play them for one-time benefits, discard them for 1 basic resource, or plant them for end-game scoring
- 14 Automa cards (solo mode)

#### Tokens & Workers
- Resources come in 5 types: Pollen, Fiber, Water (basic); Wax, Honey (advanced — you can only get these through conversion or special effects)
- 35 hibernation tokens (7 per player)
- Worker bee cubes whose strength ranges from 1 to 4 — you track this by rotating the cube to show the current face
- 1 QueenShip miniature (shared by all players, moves on the exploration map)
- Explore tokens, dance tokens, and player tracking tokens

#### Other
- Rulebook, Automa rulebook, Appendix (tile reference), Teaching guide, 5 player aids

**Checkpoint:** Make sure each player has a hive mat, a docking mat, their faction tile (basic side up), starting workers in the Active Pool, starting resources placed on hive storage spots, 7 hibernation tokens on their docking mat, and 1 seed card in hand. The shared board should show the correct side for your player count, with farm/recruit/development tiles in rows, carving tiles on Carve spaces, dance tiles on Convert spaces, and the QueenShip on its starting location.

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Theme
- Humans are long gone — honeybees have evolved into the Mellifera, a hyper-intelligent spacefaring civilization
- You control a faction of space bees, exploring planets, gathering resources, developing technologies, and creating carvings before the Dearth (winter) arrives

#### Your Turn — Pick One of Two Options
- Every turn you do exactly one thing: **Place** a worker OR **Retrieve** all your workers

#### Option A: PLACE a Worker
1. **Play seed cards (optional)** — Before placing, play any seed cards from your hand for their printed benefit, or discard them for 1 basic resource each.
2. **Place 1 worker** — Take 1 worker from your Active Pool and set it on one of the 6 action areas on the board.
3. **Bump if occupied** — If the space is already occupied, the existing worker gets bumped (see Bumping below).
4. **Resolve the action** — Your worker's strength (1–4) determines how powerful the effect is.
5. **Discard excess resources** — If you exceed your hive's storage, discard extras. Each discarded resource advances you 1 step on the Queen's Favor track.

#### Option B: RETRIEVE Workers
1. **Scoop up ALL workers** — Return every worker from the board AND your Landing Area back into your Active Pool.
2. **Strength 1–3 workers** — Collect income from one of your farm tiles (each farm pays once per retrieve), then bump that worker's strength by +1.
3. **Strength 4 workers** — They immediately hibernate (done working for good).

#### Worker Strength (1–4)
- **Numbers on cube faces** — Higher strength means stronger actions and access to exclusive strength-4 bonuses
- **Gaining strength** — Workers gain strength whenever bumped or retrieved
- **Hibernation trigger** — When a strength-4 worker would gain another level, it hibernates instead

#### Bumping — No Space Is Ever Blocked
- **Strength 1–3 worker bumped** — The owner chooses: increase +1 strength and go to Active Pool, OR keep current strength and go to Landing Area
- **Strength 4 worker bumped** — Immediately hibernates, no choice
- **Why bumping helps** — A free strength increase without spending a retrieve turn

#### Hibernation
1. **Return the worker cube** — Gone for the rest of the game.
2. **Place a hibernation token** — Into a vacant Hibernation Comb pod.
3. **Gain the pod's benefit** — Printed on the pod space.

#### Game End
- The game ends when all Hibernation Comb sectors for your player count are filled, OR when any single player places their 7th hibernation token
- Each remaining player takes 1 final turn, then you score

**Checkpoint:** Before your first turn, confirm everyone understands the Place/Retrieve choice, how bumping works (strength 1–3 gets a choice, strength 4 hibernates), and that worker strength maxes at 4 before hibernation.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Step by Step
1. Place the board with the correct side for your player count (1–3 or 4–5).
2. Shuffle planet tiles face-down. Place explore tokens on explore grid spaces. Set the QueenShip on its starting location.
3. Shuffle farm, recruit, and development tiles into 3 separate face-down stacks. Flip 3 face-up in each row on the Advance area.
4. Place carving tiles on Carve action spaces. Place dance tiles on Convert spaces.
5. Shuffle the seed card deck. Organize the resource supply within reach.
6. Each player takes a docking mat. Draft hive mats and faction tiles using a snake draft starting from last player. Place your faction tile on its basic side.
7. Each player: set tracking tokens on the score track (starting VP depends on turn order — 1st seat = 1 VP, 2nd = 2 VP, etc.), place 7 hibernation tokens on your docking mat, take starting workers and resources per your faction tile. Deal 2 seed cards to each player — keep 1, discard the other.

#### The Six Actions

**EXPLORE**
- **Movement** — Move the shared QueenShip orthogonally up to 1 space per total strength of ALL workers on Explore spaces; must end on a different planet
- **Explore token** — If the destination has one, gain it and reveal a new planet tile
- **Resources** — Place 1 basic resource on the planet if there's space, then collect all resources sitting on that planet
- **Strength-4 bonus** — Also gain the planet's printed strength-4 benefit

**ADVANCE**
- **Pick 1 tile** — Choose from the Farm, Recruit, or Development display
- **Column access** — Combined strength on Advance determines reach: 2+ = leftmost only, 3+ = left or middle, 5+ = any column
- **Pay and build** — Pay the resource cost (Farms: Fiber/Water, Recruits: Pollen, Developments: Wax), then build on an empty adjacent hex on your hive mat
- **Strength-4 bonus** — Gain 3 VP

**GROW**
- **New worker** — 1 Pollen + 1 strength point (max 4 active workers)
- **Frame extension** — 2 basic resources + 2 strength points
- **Strength-4 bonus** — Flip your faction tile to its upgraded side (the *only* way to upgrade)

**RESEARCH**
- **Draw and keep** — Draw seed cards equal to worker strength, keep exactly 1, discard the rest
- **Using kept cards** — Play later for one-time benefits, or discard for 1 basic resource
- **Strength-4 bonus** — Plant 1 seed card from your hand (tuck under hive mat for end-game scoring; slots: 2 base + 1 per frame for first 2 frames = 4 max)

**CONVERT**
- **Swaps** — Perform resource swaps up to your worker's strength number of times
- **Options** — Swap 1 basic for another, pay Pollen + Fiber for Wax, pay 2 Pollen + Water for Honey, discard/draw a seed card, or use any taught dance
- **Strength-4 bonus** — Teach a dance (once per game per player) creating a new conversion option for everyone; when opponents use your dance, you gain Queen's Favor

**CARVE**
- **Requires strength-4 worker** — Pay the honey cost for a carving tile and place it on an empty hive hex
- **End-game scoring** — Carvings score big based on specific conditions
- **Limited supply** — Never replenished once taken; real pressure to grab the best ones early

#### Hive Mat & Resource Storage
- **Tile placement** — Place tiles adjacent to existing tiles on your hexagonal hive mat
- **Build bonus icons** — Covering hexes with icons grants immediate benefits
- **Full hive** — Filling every base hex = 8 VP at endgame
- **Completed frames** — All 4 hexes filled = 8 VP at endgame per frame
- **Storage capacity** — Resources sit on designated hive spots with limited capacity; farms add more storage
- **Excess resources** — Discarded at end of turn; each discard advances Queen's Favor by 1
- **No voluntary discards** — You may NOT discard just to gain Queen's Favor

#### Queen's Favor Track
- **How to advance** — Discard excess resources or gain crown icons on various components
- **Escalating VP** — The farther you climb, the more each step is worth
- **End-game note** — Resources left in your hive at game end do NOT convert to Queen's Favor

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
- The game ends when EITHER all Hibernation Comb sectors for your player count are filled, OR any single player places their 7th hibernation token
- Each remaining player takes 1 final turn, then you score

#### In-Game VP (already tracked)
- Explore token bonuses, farm income, hibernation pod benefits, build bonuses, strength-4 bonuses, tile ability activations, seed card benefits, starting VP

#### End-Game Scoring
- **Planted seed cards** — Score based on printed conditions
- **Farms** — 1 VP each
- **Recruits** — Variable VP as printed on the tile
- **Developments** — Variable VP as printed
- **Carving tiles** — Score based on conditions (often your biggest single source)
- **Queen's Favor track** — Escalating scale; top spots are worth a lot
- **Filled hive mat** — 8 VP for all base hexes filled
- **Completed frames** — 8 VP each
- **Faction endgame ability** — From your upgraded side, if you upgraded
- **Hibernation Comb majorities** — Most tokens in a sector scores VP; ties share, rounded down

#### Tiebreaker
- Most active workers on board or docking mat; if still tied, shared victory

#### Typical Scores
- **Solid game** — Around ~100 VP
- **Above ~150** — Double-check the rules (common culprits: worker aging errors, seed card planting limits, or a game that ran too long)
- **Game length** — Roughly 18 turns per player

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Master Bump Timing
- **Free strength increase** — When your worker gets bumped, it gains +1 strength without spending a retrieve turn
- **Position strategically** — Place workers so opponents bump you at useful times
- **Offensive bumping** — Bumping an opponent's strength-4 worker forces hibernation, costing them a worker

#### Plan Your Strength-4 Turns
- **Most powerful bonuses in the game** — Carve action, faction upgrade via Grow, planting seeds via Research, teaching dances via Convert, +3 VP on Advance
- **Map out worker aging** — Squeeze maximum value from each strength-4 turn before hibernation claims the worker

#### Get Wax and Honey Without Wasting Convert Turns
- **Advanced resources** — Wax (for Developments) and Honey (for Carvings) never come directly from planets
- **Free sources** — Any tile, seed card, or explore token that hands you Wax or Honey is a massive efficiency gain
- **Prioritize these** — Whenever you spot free advanced resource sources, grab them

#### Know When to Retrieve
- **Timing is critical** — With 2–3 farm tiles, a well-timed retrieve floods you with income
- **Opportunity cost** — Every retrieve turn is a turn you're not placing a worker
- **Sweet spot** — Retrieve when you have 2–3 workers on the board and good farms to collect from

#### Complete Your Hive
- **8 VP per completion** — All base hexes filled + 8 VP per completed frame adds up fast
- **Fill gaps first** — Prioritize tiles that fill holes, even if another tile looks flashier

#### Commit to Queen's Favor or Skip It
- **Escalating returns** — Dabbling gives mediocre points
- **Go all-in** — Especially with the Retinue carving tile (worth up to 50 VP from the track alone)
- **Or ignore it** — Focus scoring elsewhere entirely

#### Seed Cards Are Powerful — But Read the Fine Print
- **Game-swinging plays** — Playing seed cards can dramatically shift outcomes
- **Efficiency tip** — Acquiring seeds outside Research (via explore tokens or dances) saves actions
- **Research rule** — Draw cards equal to strength, keep exactly 1; don't waste Research at low strength

#### Common Mistakes
- **Forgetting Research keeps only 1 card** — You draw equal to strength, but keep exactly 1. This is the most commonly misplayed rule in Apiary.
- **Voluntarily discarding for Queen's Favor** — You cannot. Only end-of-turn storage overflow triggers the track.
- **Missing the faction upgrade** — Strength-4 on Grow is the ONLY way to flip your faction tile. Don't overlook it or you'll miss your endgame ability entirely.
- **Ignoring hibernation strategy** — Choose Comb pods for both their immediate benefit AND sector majority positioning. A pod that gives you 2 Pollen isn't worth much if it loses you a 10 VP majority.
- **Late engine building** — Farms and recruits bought in Rounds 1–2 compound over many turns. Bought late, they barely pay off.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### What You Start With
- A hive mat with your faction tile on its basic side
- A docking mat with workers in your Active Pool
- Starting resources on your hive's storage spots and 1 seed card
- On the shared board: 6 action areas and the exploration map with the QueenShip

#### Early Turns (1–4)
- **Focus on Explore and Advance** — Explore moves the QueenShip to new planets for resources and explore tokens
- **Advance to build tiles** — Buy farm, recruit, or development tiles to start filling your hive
- **Farms first** — Especially valuable early because they give you both storage AND income every time you retrieve

#### Your First Retrieve
- **When to retrieve** — After placing 2–3 workers, take a Retrieve turn
- **Scoop up all workers** — For each at strength 1–3, collect income from one farm, then bump strength by +1
- **Workers come back stronger** — That's the engine warming up

#### Mid-Game (Turns 5–12)
- **Keep building your hive** — Add tiles to fill hexes
- **Start converting** — Turn basic resources into Wax (for developments) and Honey (for carvings)
- **Plan strength-4 turns** — When a worker hits 4, use its premium bonus before hibernation
- **Key strength-4 plays** — Grow upgrades your faction tile; Research lets you plant a seed card for end-game scoring

#### The Carving Phase
- **Requirements** — Honey + a strength-4 worker to use the Carve action
- **Limited supply** — Carving tiles never come back; grab ones matching your hive layout before opponents do

#### Late Game (Turns 13–18)
- **Workers hibernating regularly** — Place hibernation tokens strategically in the Comb
- **Pod selection** — Pick pods for both their immediate benefit and to secure sector majorities
- **Game end** — Triggers when the Comb fills or someone places their 7th token

#### Scoring
- Add up: in-game VP + planted seed conditions + tile VP + carving conditions + Queen's Favor + hive completion + frame completion + faction ability + Comb majorities
- **First game target** — 70–90 VP is solid; 100+ is excellent

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Hibernation Timing
- **The defining decision** — Hibernate too early and you lose actions; too late and you miss strength-4 bonuses entirely
- **Optimal pattern** — Use each worker for 2–3 placements, trigger its strength-4 bonus on the most impactful action, then let it hibernate into a strategically chosen Comb pod

#### Hive Layout Planning
- **Check build-bonus icons** — Before building, look at which covered hexes have icons; placing a tile there gives an immediate reward
- **Plan adjacencies** — Cluster farms near resource storage, land carvings on bonus hexes
- **Completion VP** — Filling every base hex (8 VP) and each frame (8 VP) is a huge chunk of your score

#### Faction Tile Asymmetry
- **Basic side** — Starting ability available from the beginning
- **Upgraded side** — Flipped via strength-4 on Grow; often defines your entire endgame
- **Study early** — Some factions score VP per tile type, others gain resources on hibernation; build toward your upgraded ability from the start

#### Resource Conversion Efficiency
- **Basic resources** — Pollen, Fiber, Water flow freely from planets
- **Advanced resources** — Wax costs Pollen + Fiber; Honey costs 2 Pollen + Water
- **Skip Convert turns** — Every advanced resource gained from tile abilities, seed cards, or explore tokens saves you a Convert turn
- **Top-tier tiles** — Advance-row recruits that produce Wax passively are among the strongest in the game

#### Carving Rush vs. Engine First
- **When to rush** — If a carving perfectly matches your hive (e.g., scores per farm when you're farm-heavy), racing to Honey + strength-4 is worth disrupting your engine
- **The trap** — Grabbing a carving that doesn't fit your build wastes Honey and a strength-4 turn for maybe 5 VP

#### Dance Floor Leverage
- **Teaching a dance** — Strength-4 on Convert, once per game; creates a conversion anyone can use
- **The trick** — Every time an opponent uses your dance, you gain Queen's Favor
- **Best dances** — Teach something opponents actually want (like a cheap Honey conversion) to passively climb the track

#### Comb Sector Majorities
- **Don't chase the best pod** — Check which sectors are contested first
- **Uncontested sectors** — Placing 2 tokens can lock up 8–12 VP in majority bonuses
- **Spread out** — A 3rd token where you already lead is usually wasteful

rulings: Research: draw cards equal to worker strength, but keep exactly 1. This is the most commonly misplayed rule.
Bumped workers at strength 1-3: owner chooses between +1 strength to Active Pool or same strength to Landing Area. Strength-4 bumped workers hibernate immediately with no choice.
You cannot voluntarily discard resources for Queen's Favor. Only end-of-turn storage overflow triggers it.
One tile per Advance action — combined strength only determines which columns you can access, not how many tiles you take.
Strength-4 on Grow is the only way to upgrade your faction tile. This is a crucial bonus often missed by new players.
Dance teaching is once per game per player. When opponents use your dance, you gain Queen's Favor.
Only basic resources (Pollen, Fiber, Water) can be placed on planets during Explore. Wax and Honey cannot.
The faction tile does NOT count as a farm, recruit, development, or carving for scoring or condition purposes.
Workers already in the Active Pool during a Retrieve turn do NOT gain strength, hibernate, or collect income.
If you begin your turn with zero workers everywhere, you gain 1 strength-1 worker free (Empty Hive Rule).
